name: ScyllaDB — 3-node cluster (calibration #9, benchANT YCSB caching benchmark)

descriptions.loaders: 2 YCSB CoreWorkload loader nodes (c5.4xlarge), each targeting 45,000 op/s (90,000 combined nominal), 50/50 read/update, uniform key distribution, ~1 KB records (10 fields x 100 bytes). Achieved combined 77,176.9 op/s (85.75% of the nominal target).
descriptions.scylla: One ScyllaDB 5.1.3 cluster (3x i4i.2xlarge, AWS us-west-2), replication factor 3, consistency QUORUM (read+write) — modeled with SDA's db.cassandra type, the CQL wire-compatible mapping the corpus already uses for a Cassandra-protocol store, as a single aggregate SDA node (the same aggregate-cluster convention the Cassandra 2017 write-ceiling / Kafka cluster entries use). connectionPool (192) is PINNED: cassandra.yaml's documented per-node concurrent_reads + concurrent_writes defaults (32+32=64, CASSANDRA_POOL_SOURCE) x 3 nodes — an INDEPENDENT scaling judgment call from the existing 2017 Cassandra write-ceiling entry (which pins 64 UNSCALED for its own cluster); this entry's tunables are NODE-scoped, so it shares nothing with that entry's type-scoped db.cassandra:throughput. connectionHeldMs is the FITTED tunable: fitted by `pnpm calibrate` to 2.45638 ms, the value that reproduces the measured 3.2328 ms blended read/update mean at the achieved 77,176.9 op/s load, given the pinned pool (post-fit residual +1.1%, grid-search granularity — see CALIBRATION-REPORT.md). `throughput` (78,164 op/s) is a derived, non-fitted display value kept consistent with pool/held by construction (== 192 / (connectionHeldMs/1000)).
